CREATE TABLE test_strings (id Int32, str String);
SELECT id FROM test_strings WHERE startsWith(str, 'Spider');
SELECT startsWith(str, 'Bat') AS starts_with_bat FROM test_strings;
SELECT str FROM test_strings WHERE startsWith(str, 'Spider') OR startsWith(str, 'iron');
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_strings WHERE startsWith(str, 'Spider') AND LENGTH(str) > 5;
SELECT id, startsWith(str, 'S') AS starts_with_s FROM (SELECT str FROM test_strings) AS subquery;
CREATE TABLE test_strings_2 (id Int32, prefix String);
SELECT ts.id FROM test_strings AS ts JOIN test_strings_2 AS ts2 ON startsWith(ts.str, ts2.prefix);
SELECT id, str FROM test_strings WHERE startsWith(str, '') = 1;
SELECT id FROM test_strings WHERE startsWith(str, 'Spider-Man') AND LENGTH(str) = 10;
SELECT str FROM test_strings WHERE NOT startsWith(str, 'X');
SELECT startsWith(str, 'Spi') FROM test_strings WHERE str IS NOT NULL;
